---
title: "edge_face_adjacency"
generated: true
description: "Pairs of faces sharing an edge (the face-adjacency topology)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.sparse.mesh`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:edge_face_adjacency -->
```python
edge_face_adjacency(faces: Int[Array, 'n_faces 3']) -> Int[Array, 'n_adjacent_pairs 2']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:edge_face_adjacency -->
Pairs of faces sharing an edge (the face-adjacency topology).

For every undirected edge incident to **exactly two** faces, emit the
`(face_a, face_b)` index pair; boundary edges (one incident face) and
non-manifold edges (three or more) contribute no pair.  This is the
topology a face-pair regulariser consumes -- e.g. a normal-consistency
penalty comparing [`face_normals`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh/face_normals.qmd) across each shared edge.

Host-side / static construction (NumPy over `faces`), like
[`mesh_k_ring_adjacency`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh/mesh_k_ring_adjacency.qmd) and `_edges_from_faces`: the result
depends only on the integer connectivity, not on vertex coordinates, so
it is computed once on the host and the returned index array then flows
through `jit` as a constant gather table.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:edge_face_adjacency -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `faces` | `Int` | `n_faces` × `3` | — | `(n_faces, 3)` vertex indices. |

**Returns** `Int` — shape `n_adjacent_pairs` × `2`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:edge_face_adjacency -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n_faces` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `3` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `n_adjacent_pairs` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `2` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
